title: Economics telemetry ingest response — replay (HTTP 200)
description: The HTTP 200 response: a batch REPLAYED under an already-seen idempotency key, with no new state written. Identical to the base ingest response in every field, and additionally binds replayed = true. The binding is not cosmetic: a 200 that carried replayed:false would tell a retrying emitter 'this was a fresh ingest' on the exact path where it was not, so it might re-emit or double-count. Making the flag a per-status const closes the disagreement in the contract rather than trusting the backend to keep body and status aligned by convention.

replayed.description: Bound true on the 200 replay path: this response is a replay of a previously-ingested batch and wrote no new state.
